req.title: Fraud alert response (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-038 governs fraud-alert lifecycle on /alerts/{id}. Key decision axes are: (1) customer response type (confirm fraud / deny fraud), (2) timing relative to the 24-hour window (well within, at exactly 24 h, past 24 h), and (3) account state transitions triggered by each path. EP partitions valid responses vs. expired alerts. BVA targets exactly T=0 min (alert just opened), T=23h59m59s (last valid second), and T=24h00m00s (first expired second). STC traces ACTIVE→CLOSED and ACTIVE→FROZEN. DT covers the confirm/deny decision matrix. NEG covers the 410 error on an already-expired alert.

tests.name: test_deny_fraud_alert_within_window_closes_alert
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status=ACTIVE and age < 24 hours
Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
No prior acknowledgement has been made on this alert
actions: Assert card reissue is NOT triggered
trace: R-FIN-038: customer confirms or denies within 24 h -> alert closed
R-FIN-038: card reissued only when fraud confirmed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_deny_fraud_alert_within_window_closes_alert(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(url, json={"response": "DENY"}, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED"
    assert body["alert"]["resolution"] == "DENIED"
    assert body["alert"].get("card_reissued", False) is False

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_triggers_card_reissue
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status=ACTIVE and age < 24 hours
Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
trace: R-FIN-038: alert closed after customer response
R-FIN-038: card reissued if fraud confirmed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_confirm_fraud_alert_triggers_card_reissue(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(url, json={"response": "CONFIRM"}, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED"
    assert body["alert"]["resolution"] == "CONFIRMED"
    assert body["alert"]["card_reissued"] is True

tests.name: test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that was created exactly 24 hours ago (age == 86400 s)
Customer has NOT responded to the alert
System auto-freeze job has executed for this alert
actions: GET /alerts/{id} to read current alert state
Assert alert.status == FROZEN or account.status == FROZEN
Assert HTTP 410 (alert expired / frozen state blocks response)
expected: Alert or account enters FROZEN state at T=24h boundary
HTTP 410 Gone on any subsequent acknowledgement attempt
trace: R-FIN-038: unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account
R-FIN-038: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold (24 h) must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h(
    expired_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    # expired_alert_fixture provides an alert aged exactly 24 h with no response
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture["id"]
    base = banking_api_base_url

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base}/alerts/{alert_id}", headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code in (200, 410)
    if get_resp.status_code == 200:
        body = get_resp.json()
        status = body.get("alert", {}).get("status") or \
                 body.get("account", {}).get("status")
        assert status == "FROZEN", f"Expected FROZEN, got {status}"

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "DENY"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Expected 410 Gone, got {patch_resp.status_code}: {patch_resp.text}"
    )

tests.name: test_respond_to_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with age > 24 hours and status=EXPIRED
Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
actions: Assert error body contains a meaningful reason code
expected: Response body contains error.code == ALERT_EXPIRED or equivalent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_respond_to_expired_alert_returns_410(
    expired_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = expired_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(url, json={"response": "DENY"}, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 410, (
        f"Expected 410 Gone for expired alert, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error", {}).get("code", "")
    assert "EXPIRED" in error_code.upper() or "GONE" in error_code.upper(), (
        f"Unexpected error code: {error_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_alert_state_transition_active_to_closed_on_valid_response
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with status=ACTIVE
expected: Intermediate ACTIVE state confirmed before PATCH
Final CLOSED state confirmed after PATCH
trace: R-FIN-038: alert closed after customer response (state lifecycle)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_alert_state_transition_active_to_closed_on_valid_response(
    active_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    alert_id = active_alert_fixture["id"]
    base = banking_api_base_url
    headers = auth_headers

    pre = requests.get(f"{base}/alerts/{alert_id}", headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["alert"]["status"] == "ACTIVE"

    patch = requests.patch(
        f"{base}/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "DENY"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert patch.status_code == 200, f"PATCH failed: {patch.text}"

    post = requests.get(f"{base}/alerts/{alert_id}", headers=headers)
    assert post.status_code == 200
    assert post.json()["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED"

tests.name: test_response_one_second_before_expiry_is_accepted
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with age == 86399 seconds (T=24h minus 1 second)
Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
expected: HTTP 200 OK — response accepted at T minus 1 second boundary
trace: R-FIN-038: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested
R-FIN-038: 24-hour window upper boundary — one second before expiry must still be valid
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_response_one_second_before_expiry_is_accepted(
    near_expiry_alert_fixture,
    auth_headers,
    banking_api_base_url,
):
    # near_expiry_alert_fixture: alert aged 86399 s (24 h - 1 s)
    alert_id = near_expiry_alert_fixture["id"]
    url = f"{banking_api_base_url}/alerts/{alert_id}"

    resp = requests.patch(url, json={"response": "DENY"}, headers=auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 at T-1s boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["alert"]["status"] == "CLOSED", (
        f"Expected CLOSED, got {body['alert']['status']}"
    )
